banner: Simulation on public benchmarks — not a forecast.

params.label: RTD price per bottle
params.source_note: RTD (ready-to-drink) matcha beverages on Indian quick-commerce apps commonly list at ₹120-180 per bottle (public benchmark range, comparable RTD tea/coffee category pricing).

params.label: Matcha cost per gram
params.source_note: Import-grade culinary matcha lands at ₹3-6/g on Indian import/specialty-ingredient listings (public benchmark range, not a single supplier quote).

params.label: Matcha grams per serve
params.source_note: 2 g of matcha powder per ~250 ml serve is a common café/RTD dosing convention (public benchmark range, not a lab-measured recipe).

params.label: Packaging cost per unit
params.source_note: Bottle, label, and cap for a small-batch RTD beverage in India commonly runs ₹15-25 per unit (public benchmark range).

params.label: Co-packing / filling cost per unit
params.source_note: Contract co-packing/filling for a small-batch beverage run commonly benchmarks ₹8-20 per unit (public benchmark range; varies with batch size and contract terms, neither observed here).

params.label: Quick-commerce commission
params.source_note: Indian quick-commerce platforms commonly take a 25-35% commission on listed price (public benchmark range across platforms and categories).

params.label: Creator-led CAC
params.source_note: Creator-led customer acquisition for F&B trial purchases in India commonly benchmarks ₹150-400 per order (public benchmark range). Noted for comparison, not modelled here: paid-performance CAC for comparable D2C F&B launches typically runs higher, often ₹400-800+, which is why creator-led acquisition is the default assumption for this simulator.

params.label: Repeat purchase rate
params.source_note: Repeat-purchase rate within the observation period for a new D2C F&B product commonly benchmarks 10-50% (public benchmark range; wide because it is highly product- and cohort-dependent, not narrowed to matcha specifically).

params.label: Orders per month per repeater
params.source_note: Repeat buyers of a habitual beverage product typically place 1-4 orders per month (public benchmark range for habitual F&B repeat cadence).

reference_scenarios.label: Defaults
reference_scenarios.description: Every slider at its default value.

reference_scenarios.label: Lean (stress case)
reference_scenarios.description: Low price, high commission, high CAC -- the pessimistic corner of the benchmark ranges.

reference_scenarios.label: Favourable
reference_scenarios.description: High price, low commission, low CAC -- the optimistic corner of the benchmark ranges.

formulas.payback_orders: payback_orders = cac_inr / contribution_per_order (None if contribution_per_order <= 0 -- CAC can never be recovered by an order that loses money)
formulas.payback_months: payback_months = payback_orders / (repeat_rate_pct/100 * orders_per_month_per_repeater) (None whenever payback_orders is None; SIMPLIFICATION -- treats the repeat cohort as the entire paying base placing orders_per_month_per_repeater orders/month, not a full cohort-decay/LTV model)

benchmarks_note: All defaults are author-asserted public benchmark ranges (see each param's source_note), not measured data.
